rationale.R1.1: Artifact presents an explicit thesis (chemistry-agnostic SoH via raw telemetry), a gap statement (model-free, no calibration), and a contribution claim (TCN+attention outperforms baselines); 'What do you think?' plausibly invites evaluation of whether the framing holds.
rationale.R1.2: Several internal tensions exist — e.g., IC peaks at ~3.42 V and ~3.58 V are attributed to graphite staging transitions yet the system spans LFP (plateau chemistry), which has different IC signatures; also, 'chemistry-agnostic' is asserted while auxiliary tokens are IC-peak locations that are chemistry-specific. These could be load-bearing logical failures.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete RMSE numbers are quoted (1.8 % vs 3.4 %, 2.9 %, 2.5 %) and a claim about 'largest gains on LFP subset' and 'strongest single-feature correlation' are made without accompanying statistical characterisation; delegation plausibly asks whether these claims are adequately evidenced.
rationale.R1.4: TCN+self-attention for battery SoH and IC-peak features are not individually novel in the 2024–2026 literature; whether the combination constitutes a meaningful delta is a natural target of 'What do you think?'

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking primarily for a technical critique of the methodology and evidentiary claims (logical soundness, evidence-claim alignment), rather than for an assessment of the novelty and contribution positioning relative to prior work?
